data.title: Cake Group’s Bake to stop providing services in Singapore from May
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bake.io, a product of Singapore's crypto investment institution Cake Group, announced in an email to users that it will stop providing services in Singapore from May 1, 2025. 
Previously, the platform was handed over to new management. According to a statement released by the London Stock Exchange on January 2, 2025, Cake Group sold Bake to GSTechnologies Ltd., a fintech-focused company, last month. 
The details of the transaction are unclear. GSTechnologies' financial data showed that as of September 2024, the company held only $3.26 million in cash and digital assets. In January this year, the company announced that it had raised $626,000. 
Cake Group, whose main business is Bake, once claimed to hold $1 billion in assets and revenue of $631 million in 2021. The latest financial data submitted by Cake Group in 2023 showed that its assets were $169 million. It is not clear how much assets Cake Group has left after selling Bake. (TechinAsia)

data.title: Tether Expands Into AI With New Applications
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Tether, the world's largest stablecoin issuer, is advancing its efforts in artificial intelligence with several applications currently under development. Tether's AI division, Tether Data, is working on AI Translate, AI Voice Assistant, and AI Bitcoin Wallet Assistant, as announced by CEO Paolo Ardoino on February 4, 2025. 

Ardoino revealed that Tether plans to launch an open-source AI SDK platform built on Bare, a JavaScript runtime by Holepunch, which will be compatible with various hardware, including embedded devices, mobile phones, laptops, and powerful servers. The applications are designed to operate locally on any device, ensuring full privacy and self-custody for both data and financial transactions. 

A demonstration of the AI Bitcoin Wallet Assistant showcased a user interacting with a "Payment Agent" to retrieve their BTC wallet address, check their balance, and send BTC to a contact from their address book. This process was executed through an AI chatbot interface, highlighting the autonomous capabilities of the application. 

Tether's AI Translate functions as a simple chatbot translation tool, while the AI Voice Assistant responds to voice inputs instead of text. Tether's interest in AI became apparent in 2023 when it acquired a stake in Northern Data Group, a European crypto miner specializing in cloud computing and generative AI. 

In March, Tether expanded its AI operations by recruiting top-tier talent globally, aiming to push the boundaries of AI technology and set new industry standards for innovation and utility. Ardoino mentioned in December that the draft for Tether's AI platform site was ready, with a launch targeted for the end of Q1 2025. 

On January 31, Tether reported record-breaking profits of $13 billion for 2024 and disclosed a substantial stockpile of US government bonds, valued at approximately $113 billion. Tether's stablecoin, USDT, remains the third-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, reaching an all-time high of $141 billion following the issuance of an additional billion dollars on February 5.

data.title: Aerodrome Public Product Fund has repurchased 1.5 million AERO and locked them as veAERO
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Aerodrome announced that its Public Product Fund (PGF) has repurchased 1.5 million AERO and locked them as veAERO to increase PGF voting rights.

data.title: Rebecca Simmonds appointed new CEO of Walrus Foundation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Decentralized storage and data availability protocol Walrus announced on X that Rebecca Simmonds (@rj_simmonds) has become the new CEO of the Walrus Foundation. As the Web3 storage field heats up and the mainnet is about to be launched, she will help Walrus expand, innovate, and break boundaries.

data.title: Burwick Law, the law firm that sued pump.fun, asked the platform to remove tokens that used its IP
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News US law firm Burwick Law said in a statement on February 5 that it and Wolf Popper had sent a cease and desist letter to pump.fun, demanding the "immediate removal" of Dog Shit Going NoWhere (DOGSHIT2) and other tokens that "impersonated our company" by using intellectual property (including its logo and name) without permission.
According to pump.fun, multiple users created tokens using the names and logos of Burwick Law and Wolf Popper in various configurations. In the ongoing lawsuit against pump.fun, some tokens also used the names and portraits of Burwick Law employees and one of its clients.
The law firm said: "Burwick Law confirmed that pump.fun has the technical ability to delete these tokens, but chose not to take action, despite the obvious financial and legal risks to the public."
Earlier in January, Burwick Law, a US law firm specializing in the field of cryptocurrency, said it was considering filing a lawsuit on behalf of investors who suffered losses from pump.fun.
Burwick said the move came after "months" of working with ordinary people who had lost "significant amounts of money" to unfulfilled promises on platforms such as Meme Coin, Rug Pull and pump.fun. The statement added: "Over the past few months, pump.fun has collected hundreds of millions of dollars in fees while the platform has been rife with illegal drug use, self-harm, racism, anti-Semitism, obscenity, violence and other antisocial behavior."

data.title: Ethereum Gas Fees Drop to New Low
data.description: According to Foresight News, data from Etherscan indicates that Ethereum gas fees have decreased to 1.039 Gwei today. The estimated cost for a swap transaction is currently $0.91.

data.title: ETH block options on Deribit buys put and call options at $2,600 at the end of June
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Lin Chen, head of Asia-Pacific business at Deribit, wrote on X that today’s largest ETH bulk option transaction was: a user paid $876,000 to purchase $2,600 put and call options at the end of June, for a total of 1,000 ETH on one side.
